gap_method: Onset of pi-pi* absorption (UV-vis); review reports the electronic bandgap as 1.6-1.7 eV with lambda_max ca. 610 nm for PEDT film
citation: Groenendaal, L.; Jonas, F.; Freitag, D.; Pielartzik, H.; Reynolds, J. R. Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) and Its Derivatives: Past, Present, and Future. Adv. Mater. 2000, 12 (7), 481-494. Exact wording: 'PEDT exhibits an electronic bandgap, defined as the onset of the p-p* absorption, of 1.6-1.7 eV and a lmax of ca. 610 nm.' Value recorded is the lower bound of the stated 1.6-1.7 eV range. No film p-doping onset potential with a reference electrode is reported in this source for parent PEDOT.
flags: reference '' not cleanly resolvable to a pinned shift -> converted omitted

gap_method: Onset of pi-pi* transition (UV-vis); source groups PEDOT and PProDOT as thiophene counterparts with band gaps of 1.6-1.7 eV
citation: Reynolds, J. R. et al. (University of Florida). Electrochromic polymers and polymer electrochromic devices. US Patent 6,791,738 B2 (2004). Patent text states the band gaps for PEDOP (2.0 eV) and PProDOP (2.2 eV) 'are higher than their thiophene counterparts ... which have band gaps of 1.6-1.7 eV', defining the band gap as the onset of the pi-pi* transition; PProDOT is grouped with these thiophene counterparts. Value recorded is the lower bound of the stated 1.6-1.7 eV range. No single parent-PProDOT film doping/oxidation onset potential with a verbatim reference electrode could be sourced to a locatable primary reference.
flags: reference '' not cleanly resolvable to a pinned shift -> converted omitted

gap_method: Onset of pi-pi* transition (UV-vis)
citation: Schottland, P.; Zong, K.; Gaupp, C. L.; Thompson, B. C.; Thomas, C. A.; Giurgiu, I.; Hickman, R.; Abboud, K. A.; Reynolds, J. R. Poly(3,4-alkylenedioxypyrrole)s: Highly Stable Electronically Conducting and Electrochromic Polymers. Macromolecules 2000, 33 (19), 7051-7061. Band gap for PEDOP reported as 2.0 eV (onset of pi-pi* transition). Abstract: this class of polymers exhibits 'especially low oxidation potentials from ca. -0.6 to -0.4 V vs Fc/Fc+ (equivalent to -0.15 to +0.05 V vs SCE) as desired for ambient stability of the doped and conducting states.' The doping_onset value recorded (-0.6 V vs Fc/Fc+) is the onset (low-potential bound) of the reported oxidation-potential range stated for the poly(3,4-alkylenedioxypyrrole) family that includes PEDOP; reported verbatim, not converted.

gap_method: Onset of pi-pi* transition (UV-vis)
citation: Schottland, P.; Zong, K.; Gaupp, C. L.; Thompson, B. C.; Thomas, C. A.; Giurgiu, I.; Hickman, R.; Abboud, K. A.; Reynolds, J. R. Poly(3,4-alkylenedioxypyrrole)s: Highly Stable Electronically Conducting and Electrochromic Polymers. Macromolecules 2000, 33 (19), 7051-7061. Band gap for the propylene-bridged analog PProDOP reported as 2.2 eV (onset of pi-pi* transition). Abstract reports the family exhibits 'especially low oxidation potentials from ca. -0.6 to -0.4 V vs Fc/Fc+ (equivalent to -0.15 to +0.05 V vs SCE).' The doping_onset value recorded (-0.6 V vs Fc/Fc+) is the onset (low-potential bound) of the reported oxidation-potential range for the poly(3,4-alkylenedioxypyrrole) family that includes PProDOP; reported verbatim, not converted. Note: PProDOP monomer (ProDOP) is not in the supplied library list; included because it is co-reported with PEDOP and is a focus-adjacent dioxypyrrole.

gap_method: Optical band gap from extrapolation of the linear part of the (alpha*h*nu)^2 vs h*nu plot to (alpha*h*nu)^2 = 0 (Tauc-type extrapolation); polymer film; lambda_max = 475 nm (Table 2).
citation: D. Sheberla, S. Patra, Y. H. Wijsboom, S. Sharma, Y. Sheynin, A.-E. Haj-Yahia, A. H. Barak, O. Gidron, M. Bendikov, 'Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans', Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371. DOI: 10.1039/C4SC02664K. Polymer optical band gap (Table 2) for PT3 = 2.02 eV. Note: the paper reports polymer p-doping behavior via the half-maximal-conductance potential Ehc (PT3 = 0.87 V vs Ag/AgCl-wire), which is NOT an oxidation-onset potential; the 0.95 V vs Ag/AgCl-wire value in Table 1 is the MONOMER (terthiophene oligomer) oxidation onset, not the polymer doping onset. Hence no polymer doping-onset value is reported here.

gap_method: Tauc plot: extrapolation of linear part of (alpha*h*nu)^2 vs h*nu to (alpha*h*nu)^2 = 0; lambda_max 466 nm

gap_method: UV-vis absorption onset of neutral polymer (lambda_onset = 893 nm); Eg = 1240/lambda_onset
citation: Yadav, P.; Naqvi, S.; Patra, A. Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxyselenophene): effect of solvent and electrolyte on electrodeposition, optoelectronic and electrochromic properties. RSC Advances 2020, 10 (21), 12395-12406. For PEDOS deposited in TBAClO4/MeCN: optical band gap 1.38 eV from absorption onset (lambda_onset 893 nm, Table 3); polymer oxidation onset Eox,poly,onset = -0.68 V vs Ag/Ag+ (Table 2). citation: Thombare, J. V.; Shinde, S. K.; Lohar, G. M.; Chougale, U. M.; Dhasade, S. S.; Dhaygude, H. D.; Relekar, B. P.; Fulari, V. J. Band gap of electrodeposited polypyrrole films = 1.95 eV (H2SO4), 1.92 eV (oxalic acid), 1.79 eV (p-toluenesulfonic acid) from UV-vis. Value reported is for the as-deposited (p-doped) film, not a strictly neutral-state onset.
